prompt: Read the annotated image at /home/mia/Pictures/Screenshot_20260417_071059.png and investigate the ava-pi-coding-agent TUI widgets in packages/widgets to identify duplicate or overlapping widget rendering that eats terminal space, then optimize what the TUI extension displays.

primary.target: TUI extension display output in packages/widgets

secondary.target: duplicate or overlapping widget rendering consuming terminal space
which widgets register the same extension slot (status line, above/below editor)
which widget content is redundant vs unique across ava-ceremony, ava-echo, ava-presence
widget source files to remove redundant rendering

assumptions: The screenshot contains visual annotations marking the problematic TUI areas
Widget source files exist in packages/widgets/src/ (CLAUDE.md notes they may not yet be implemented)
Multiple widgets are currently rendering to overlapping terminal zones
Reducing terminal space consumption is the desired outcome, not adding new widgets

updates: packages/widgets/src/ — one or more widget index.tsx files with reduced/deduplicated rendering
packages/widgets/index.ts — possibly if widget registration needs consolidation

east.text: Understand what the screenshot reveals about current TUI layout and which zones are overcrowded
Envision a TUI that uses terminal space efficiently with no redundant ceremony/presence data repeated across widgets

south.text: Audit each widget's registered extension slots (statusLine, widgetAboveEditor, widgetBelowEditor)
Compare rendered content across ava-ceremony, ava-presence, and ava-echo for overlapping data
Identify which presence/ceremony fields are rendered more than once across widget set

west.text: Validate that optimized widgets still surface essential ceremony phase, direction, and presence state
Confirm no widget is left rendering an empty or near-empty zone after deduplication

north.text: Edit widget source files to eliminate duplicate rendering across overlapping zones
Consolidate status-line content into a single widget if multiple widgets compete for that slot

actionStack.text: Read the annotated screenshot to understand visually which TUI zones are problematic
Glob packages/widgets/src/ to enumerate all widget source files that exist
Read each widget index.tsx to identify registered extension slots and rendered content
Map which slots (statusLine, widgetAboveEditor, widgetBelowEditor) are claimed by multiple widgets
Identify content fields rendered redundantly across more than one widget
Decide which widget owns each unique piece of content and which renderings to remove
Edit widget source files to remove or consolidate duplicate rendering
Verify resulting widget set covers all essential ceremony/presence signals without repetition

ambiguities.text: Widget source .tsx files may not exist yet (CLAUDE.md notes this uncertainty)
ambiguities.suggestion: Glob first to confirm which files are present before reading

ambiguities.text: 'Optimize what the TUI extension displays' — unclear if scope is content reduction, layout consolidation, or both
ambiguities.suggestion: Use screenshot annotations as the authoritative guide for what to remove

ambiguities.text: No explicit constraint on which widgets must be preserved vs which can be fully removed
ambiguities.suggestion: Preserve ava-echo (diary persistence) and ava-ceremony (ceremony tracking) as highest-value; treat ava-presence as the deduplication candidate
